display_name: No debug output does not prove the event isn't happening — the debug may never see it
context: Running a debug to confirm a suspected event and seeing no debug output at all
observation: A debug is enabled but produces no output even though the suspected condition appears to be occurring
naive_interpretation: The absence of debug output proves the event is not happening, so the hypothesis is wrong
correct_interpretation: Debugging only reports what the control-plane/CPU path actually processes. Hardware-forwarded (CEF/hardware-switched) traffic never reaches the CPU, so a packet-level debug will show nothing even while the traffic flows at line rate. Output can also be missing because a debug condition/ACL filtered it, logging to the current terminal isn't enabled, or rate-limiting suppressed it. No output is inconclusive, not disproof — verify the debug can actually observe the path before drawing a conclusion.
why: Production traffic is hardware/CEF-switched and bypasses the CPU, so packet debugs (which run on the CPU path) never see it
Conditional debugging (debug condition / debug ACLs) restricts output to matching criteria; a mismatched filter yields silence
Debug output goes to the console/monitor only when logging for that destination is enabled (e.g. terminal monitor), otherwise it is invisible on the current session
Internal logging rate-limiting can drop debug messages under load, producing gaps that look like absence

confirmation_commands.purpose: Confirm which debugs and conditions are actually active so a filter isn't silently excluding the event
Enable debug/log output to the current VTY session so existing output is not simply unseen
Check whether CPU-bound copies of traffic are rate-limited, which would starve a packet debug of samples

traps: Concluding a hypothesis is wrong because a packet debug for hardware-switched traffic stayed silent
Forgetting terminal monitor / logging destination, so debug output exists but is not shown on the current session
Leaving a stale debug condition or ACL that filters out the very traffic being investigated
Running heavy debugs that get rate-limited under load and reading the gaps as absence of the event
